content.problemStatement:
There are **N** minions who are competing in an election of the president of the ACM (Association of Cute Minions). They are standing in a line in the order from minion 1 to minion **N**. For each **i** (1 ≤ **i** ≤ **N**), the **i**-th minion has an influence level of **Si**.

A single minion may cast any number of votes. Minion **j** will vote for minion **i** (**i** ≠ **j**) if and only if the influence level of the **j**-th minion is greater than or equal to the sum of influence levels of all the minions standing between them (excluding the **i**-th and **j**-th minion).

Your task is to find the number of votes received by each minion.
constraints: sum of N over all test cases won't exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N denoting the number of minions. The second line contains N space-separated integers S1, S2, ..., SN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing N space-separated integers. For each valid i, the i-th of these integers should denote the number of votes the i-th minion will get.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: The first minion will get only a vote of the second minion. The second minion will get votes of the first and third minion. The third minion will get votes of the first, second and fourth minion. The fourth minion will get votes of the second and third minion.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can iterate over each minion and check which other minions will vote for them. We can do this by comparing the influence level of the current minion with the sum of influence levels of all the minions standing between them (excluding the current minion). If the influence level of the other minion is greater than or equal to the sum, then the other minion will vote for the current minion.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) — because for each test case, we are iterating over each minion and for each minion, we are iterating over all the other minions to check if they will vote for the current minion.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — because we need to store the influence levels of all the minions and the number of votes each minion will get.
